text: During the surgery, the transverse mesocolon is retracted caudally, revealing the underlying structures and facilitating access to the hernia sac containing small bowel loops.
The surgeon retracts the small bowel, revealing a hernia orifice located on the left side of the duodenal-jejunal junction.
The surgeon reduces the small bowel loops from the hernia sac into the peritoneal cavity, reaching the area just after the fourth part of the duodenum.
The surgical field shows no signs of ischemia in the small bowel loops within the left para-duodenal hernia sac.
The surgeon evaluates the hernia sac, revealing a small amount of serohemorrhagic fluid within the cavity.
The surgeon uses a laparoscopic suturing device to close the hernia orifice at the left side of the duodenal-jejunal junction with a non-absorbable suture.
The surgeon uses the purse string method to close the hernia orifice entirely, taking serosal bites from the jejunal segment.
The surgeon completes the operation after verifying the closure of the hernia orifice.
